strengths: Extensive backend and distributed-systems expertise evidenced by high-scale, low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec) and real-time data platforms at GISCollective

Hands-on experience with event-driven architectures and Kubernetes deployments in a production environment (GISCollective, re:cinq), aligning with the need for modular, multi-agent agentic AI frameworks
Strong track record of scaling engineering practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE Capital) and leading technical initiatives, fitting the leadership and governance requirements
concerns: Limited direct fintech experience, though credit-risk and payment systems are not explicitly mentioned in prior roles; fintech-adjacent context exists but is not deeply commercial
No explicit mention of agentic AI systems in production beyond tooling at re:cinq; core agent architecture responsibilities may require extrapolation
Publicly documented work is predominantly full-stack or backend; depth in AI-native fintech integration (e.g., real-time credit scoring, liquidity optimization) is not directly verifiable
response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo brings a strong foundation in high-scale, event-driven architectures and distributed systems, critical for building modular multi-agent frameworks and real-time AI systems. Her hands-on experience with Kubernetes, low-latency microservices, and disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD) directly supports the technical and leadership requirements. While fintech-specific experience is lighter, her ability to rapidly integrate new domains and scale teams suggests high upside for mastering algorithmic working-capital and credit-risk systems.
